title: EvidenceBundle
description: A portable, offline-verifiable export of a correlation's evidence (CHP v0.2). Signed bundles are tamper-evident; hash-chain bundles detect mutation/reordering.

host_id.description: The host that produced the evidence.

canonicalization.description: Named canonicalization scheme for the bundle-header signature (chp-v0.2.md section 2). A verifier dispatches the serializer on this value. chp-jcs-v1 is RFC 8785 JCS (proposal 0015).

events.description: Ordered evidence events, each carrying its content_hash and prev_hash.

root_hash.description: SHA256 over each event's content_hash in sequence order.

completeness.description: Non-omission claim (chp-v0.2.md §12, proposal 0018): the bundle asserts it is the complete correlation as of a global sequence. Optional; when present it is bound into the signed bundle header. Audited against a witnessed store head's leaves.

correlation_id.description: The correlation this bundle claims to complete.

as_of_sequence.description: Host assertion: no events for this correlation exist through this global store sequence.

head_hash.description: content_hash of the tail (last) event — equals the correlation's leaf in a store head that witnessed completeness.

public_key.description: Base64 ed25519 public key (present on signed bundles).

signature.description: ed25519 signature over root_hash (present on signed bundles).

host_identity.description: Self-signed host-identity attestation (chp-v0.2.md §3). 'anchors' is omitted entirely when empty (§3.1 byte rule).

anchors.description: External trust roots vouching for this key, e.g. {"type":"domain","domain":"acme.example"}. MUST be omitted when empty.
